pattern: Only the left-to-right sequence of leaf values matters, so collapsing each tree down to just its leaves reduces the comparison to a simple sequence equality.
hints: Walk each tree and record only the values of nodes that have no children, in left-to-right order.
The internal shape of either tree is irrelevant; two very differently shaped trees can still produce the same leaf sequence.
Compare the two collected sequences directly for equality, including their lengths.

edge_inputs.description: differently shaped trees with the same leaf sequence
same values arranged so the leaf order differs
two single-node trees with equal value
one tree is a skewed chain collapsing to one leaf, the other is that same single leaf, matching despite very different depth
same two leaf values but reversed left-to-right order
leaf count matches but one tree's rightmost leaf value differs because of where a node's children attach
single-node trees at opposite ends of the value range

stress.description: two independently built random general binary trees, comparison outcome not constrained
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    def rand_tree(n, root_val):
        nodes = {0: [root_val, None, None]}
        open_slots = [(0, 'left'), (0, 'right')]
        next_id = 1
        for _ in range(1, n):
            idx = rng.randrange(len(open_slots))
            parent_id, side = open_slots.pop(idx)
            nid = next_id
            next_id += 1
            nodes[nid] = [rng.randint(0, 200), None, None]
            if side == 'left':
                nodes[parent_id][1] = nid
            else:
                nodes[parent_id][2] = nid
            open_slots.append((nid, 'left'))
            open_slots.append((nid, 'right'))
        from collections import deque
        out = []
        q = deque([0])
        while q:
            cur = q.popleft()
            if cur is None:
                out.append(None)
                continue
            val, l, r = nodes[cur]
            out.append(val)
            q.append(l)
            q.append(r)
        while out and out[-1] is None:
            out.pop()
        return out
    n1 = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 200)))
    n2 = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 200)))
    t1 = rand_tree(n1, rng.randint(0, 200))
    t2 = rand_tree(n2, rng.randint(0, 200))
    return [t1, t2]
